name: Ramp entry
description: A sloped ramp that resolves a vertical offset between the surrounding ground and the building's finished floor by walking the visitor up a gentle incline rather than a stair. Read as a single continuous paved surface, ideally one-in-six or shallower, terminating at the main door or temple portico. Three idiomatic uses: Mediterranean villas absorbing hillside grade so the loggia stays level; Egyptian and Greek temples staging a slow ceremonial ascent that elevates the cella above the worshipper; and modern accessible entries where the ramp replaces or parallels a stair without breaking the building line. Optional flanking low parapets, occasional landings every few blocks of run, and a paved threshold cell where the ramp meets the door.
